subagent_findings.surface_area_mapper: minor_break — two minor breaks: getDesktopPluginConfig return type change, PluginKind union widening
subagent_findings.convergence_checker: minor_deviation — triple-duplicated validation pattern, no cross-reference to PG migration rename
subagent_findings.coupling_auditor: blocking — test duplicates production code; dead dialog-install-plugin.tsx; duplicate PluginConfigEntry type
subagent_findings.debuggability_forecaster: concerning — 120 of 163 min debug time on 3 silent failure scenarios; asyncWrite data loss risk
subagent_findings.error_trace_auditor: silent_majority — 7/15 error paths completely silent; ErrorBoundary hides ALL render errors; claim atom #5 false
subagent_findings.isolation_tester: adequate — 4/9 changes isolatable; 13 bun tests for validation; 6 Electron/UI items inherently non-isolatable

blocking_findings.finding: Test replicates isValidPluginConfigEntry and validateAndFilterPluginConfigs verbatim because production functions at ipc.ts:84,90 are not exported. Claim atom 11 is unsupported — tests verify copies, not actual production code. If production code changes, tests still pass silently.
blocking_findings.repair: Add `export` keyword to both functions in ipc.ts, then update test to `import { isValidPluginConfigEntry, validateAndFilterPluginConfigs } from '../src/main/ipc'`.

blocking_findings.finding: loadConfigs() is called via IIFE (`;(async () => await loadConfigs())()`) during component render instead of inside `onMount()`. In SolidJS, calling signal setters (setConfigs, setDropped, setLoading) during render causes cascading re-renders before DOM is mounted. If window.api is undefined during early mount, this produces a runtime error with no recovery path.
blocking_findings.repair: Wrap loadConfigs() in `onMount(() => { loadConfigs() })`. Initialize `loading` signal as `true` so the component shows the loading indicator until data arrives.

blocking_findings.finding: DialogInstallPlugin component is exported but never imported by any module. The install form is inlined in DialogManagePlugins via ViewMode toggle. This is dead code — adds 155 lines of maintenance surface with zero runtime reachability.
blocking_findings.repair: Either (a) wire DialogInstallPlugin into a real consumer, or (b) delete the file. Option (b) is recommended — the inline form in DialogManagePlugins already handles the full install UX.

blocking_findings.finding: ErrorBoundary uses invisible fallback `<div style={{display:'none'}} />`. SolidJS ErrorBoundary does NOT console.error caught errors when using a static element fallback. Plugin render errors are invisible to BOTH user AND developer. This is the most dangerous silent failure in the entire plan.
blocking_findings.repair: Use function form: `<ErrorBoundary fallback={(err) => { console.error('Plugin slot render error:', err); return <div style={{display:'none'}} /> }}>`.

blocking_findings.finding: Claim atom #5: 'Each plugin can store/retrieve per-plugin config via dedicated store' is FALSE. The pluginStore in loadEntry is an in-memory `Map<string, unknown>` — not persisted anywhere. Config is lost on page reload or app restart. The electron-store 'desktop-plugin-config' only stores plugin metadata (name, path, enabled), not per-plugin config data.
blocking_findings.repair: Either (a) downgrade/remove claim atom #5 — clarify per-plugin config is ephemeral in-memory only, or (b) implement persistent per-plugin config via namespaced sub-store for each plugin.

weak_findings.finding: Silent catch blocks across 5 locations. On IPC failure, errors are swallowed with `catch { setConfigs([]) }` or `catch { // ignore }`. Developer sees empty plugin list or stale state with zero diagnostic trail. Two openFilePicker handlers are marked `// ignore` — the worst offenders.
weak_findings.repair: Add `console.error()` with descriptive context to each catch block. Replace `// ignore` comments with meaningful logging. Consider setting an error signal that surfaces a toast notification.

weak_findings.finding: `.catch(() => undefined)` silently swallows IPC rejection. If getDesktopPluginConfig handler throws, the error is invisible — no console.warn, no diagnostic. Developer debugging 'why are no plugins loading' has zero error trail.
weak_findings.repair: Change to `.catch((reason) => { console.error('getDesktopPluginConfig rejected:', reason); return undefined })`.

weak_findings.finding: IPC handlers for get/set-desktop-plugin-config lack try/catch protection. If getStore('desktop-plugin-config') throws (corrupt file, disk I/O), the rejection propagates to the renderer as an unhandled promise rejection. session-export-data handler (ipc.ts:231) uses try/catch → return {error}, creating an inconsistent error treatment pattern within the same file.
weak_findings.repair: Add try/catch to both plugin IPC handlers: `try { ... } catch (e) { console.error(...); return { configs: [], dropped: 0 } }`. File a follow-up to apply same pattern to MCP and agents handlers.

weak_findings.finding: loadAll uses `Promise.all(plugins.map(loadEntry))`. One bad plugin path crashes ALL plugin loading. Plugins B, C, D never load despite having valid configurations. Additionally, `this.loaded.add(entry.name)` at line 88 runs BEFORE await import(), so a failed import permanently prevents retry.
weak_findings.repair: Replace `Promise.all` with `Promise.allSettled` + per-plugin error accumulation. Move `this.loaded.add(entry.name)` to after successful `await import()` completes.

weak_findings.finding: Debuggability-forecaster: electron-store defaults to asyncWrite=true (since v10). The set-desktop-plugin-config handler returns success before the disk write completes. If the write fails (disk full, permission change), the UI shows success but next restart loses all changes. Data loss is invisible until app restart.
weak_findings.repair: Set `asyncWrite: false` in Store config for 'desktop-plugin-config', or add a readback after write: verify `store.get('configs')` matches expected data.

weak_findings.finding: submitInstall uses wrong i18n key. When installMode is 'file' and path is empty, it shows invalidNpmName instead of invalidFilePath. The early-return check does not branch on installMode. dialog-install-plugin.tsx:47-54 correctly branches on mode.
weak_findings.repair: Change line 87-90 to branch on mode: `installMode() === 'npm' ? t('...invalidNpmName') : t('...invalidFilePath')`.

weak_findings.finding: Triple-duplicated validation pattern with two different return conventions: validateAndFilterAgents returns `unknown[]`, validateAndFilterPluginConfigs returns `{configs, dropped}`, validateAndFilterMcpServers returns `unknown[]`. The `{configs, dropped}` shape is architecturally superior but the plan didn't backport it to the other handlers.
weak_findings.repair: Extract a shared `validateAndFilter<T>(raw, guard)` utility returning `{ items: T[]; dropped: number }`. Backport to agents and MCP handlers for consistent return conventions.

informational_findings.finding: PluginConfigEntry type is defined in two places: preload/types.ts (canonical export) and plugin-loader.ts (local interface). dialog components correctly import from preload/types.ts; plugin-loader.ts has a duplicate that will silently diverge if the canonical type changes.
informational_findings.repair: Replace local interface in plugin-loader.ts with `import type { PluginConfigEntry } from '../preload/types'`.

informational_findings.finding: No cross-reference to concurrent PG migration rename work (pg-runtime-wiring-layer-fix plan). Both plans mutate the same files — one renames SqliteMigrationProgress, the other adds plugin methods.
informational_findings.repair: Add pg-runtime-wiring-layer-fix as a coordinating dependency in the plan's metadata.

informational_findings.finding: getDesktopPluginConfig return type changed from `Promise<any>` to `Promise<{configs: PluginConfigEntry[]; dropped: number}>`. Minor break — runtime consumers that treated result as flat array get `undefined` for `.configs`. The plan repaired the only known consumer (plugin-loader.ts:39).
informational_findings.repair: Document the break in release notes. Add a type-level compatibility test.

conditions: Must fix the test isolation issue: export validation functions from ipc.ts, import them in test file instead of duplicating. Required for claim atom 11 to hold.
Must move loadConfigs() call into onMount() in dialog-manage-plugins.tsx to prevent SolidJS render-timing errors.
Must either wire or delete dialog-install-plugin.tsx (dead export of 155 lines).
Must fix ErrorBoundary in plugin-context.tsx:64 to log caught errors instead of hiding them silently.
Must correct or remove claim atom #5 — per-plugin config is in-memory only, not persisted.
Must fix wrong i18n key in dialog-manage-plugins.tsx:87-90 (invalidNpmName when file mode).
follow_up_issues: Unify validateAndFilter* return conventions across all three handler pairs (agents, MCP, plugins) in ipc.ts
Add try/catch to all domain-specific IPC handlers (agents, MCP, plugins) consistent with session-export-data pattern
Create a shared type-authority module for preload↔renderer boundary types to prevent PluginConfigEntry-style duplication
Add asyncWrite verification to electron-store writes for plugin config to prevent silent data loss
Replace Promise.all with allSettled in plugin-loader.ts loadAll for resilient multi-plugin loading
Add PG migration rename plan as coordinating dependency to prevent import-line collisions
